nodes.title: Experiencing NZ Educ & Culture
nodes.description: Travel course that offers current educators and graduate students the opportunity to explore English and Maori educational facilities in and around Auckland, New Zealand. Educators will explore New Zealand culture, the Ministry of Education's current educational direction, and the cultural integration of Maori and Pakeha education. Participants will visit approximately nine different primary/intermediate schools. These schools will reflect the various decile levels present in New Zealand Education.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission only. Open to Graduate/PACE students.

nodes.title: SU:Social Educ &Social Studies
nodes.description: Social Studies is the study of how humans have interacted with their environment and one another over time. The goal of teachers is to prepare students to be contributing and engaged citizens. Course covers how to teach social studies to children in a way that prepares them to be active citizens in a democratic and sustainable society. Focuses on elementary social studies standards, content, strategies, and resources. Pre/co-requisites: Teacher Education:Elementary Education major; EDEL 3550, EDEL 3580.
nodes.other_requirements: Teacher Education:Elementary Education major; ELK6 3rd yr, 4th yr; Admit to Teacher Education; 2.75 overall GPA requirement; Concurrent with EDEL 3550 and EDEL 3580.

nodes.title: Intrvn in the clssrm: Math SL
nodes.description: See Schedule of Course for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: 6 weeks of time in the community dates TBD.

nodes.title: Language Arts&Literacy Skills
nodes.description: Cognitive research base for the social context of children's learning. Methods of language arts as literate activity. Emphasis on emergence of literacy in the child of special need. Prerequisites: Admission to Elementary Teacher Education Program; concurrent with EDEL 3780, EDEL 3750, EDEL 2570.
nodes.other_requirements: Admission to Elementary Teacher Education Program; ELK6 2nd yr, Admit to Teacher Education

nodes.title: SL:Teaching Science forMeaning
nodes.description: Teaching K-6 science through inquiry. Use of constructivist pedagogy to develop lessons and activities that develop concepts from physical, earth, and life sciences. Pre/co-requisites: Admission to the Elementary Education Program; concurrent with EDEL 3550 & EDEL 3570.
nodes.other_requirements: Admission to the Elementary Education Program; ELK6 3rd yr, 4th yr; Admit to Teacher Education; 2.75 overall GPA requirement; Co-located with EDEC 2510

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Soc, Hst & Phil Found of Educ
nodes.description: Critical examination of central educational/social issues and values with special emphasis on the struggle for justice and equality. Themes include schooling and social class, race, and gender; the purposes of education; and the responsibilities of teachers. Prerequisite: Completion of a 2000 level course.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Child Development
nodes.description: The biological, psychological, and social growth and development of children and their relationships with family, peers, and institutions.
nodes.other_requirements: Degree students only

nodes.title: Plng, Adptg, Dlvring Lit Instr
nodes.description: Extending and refining knowledge about reading, writing instruction, and assessment. Students will review literature, implement classroom-based assessment strategies, and develop lessons and units for literacy instruction. Prerequisites: EDEL 3750, EDEL 3760; Elementary Education Grades K-6) major; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Elementary Education Grades K-6) major; minimum Junior standing; instructor permission; ELK6 3rd or 4th yr; Taken concurrently with EDEL 3750, EDEL 3780, and EDEL 3770. Take prior to student teaching.

nodes.title: Teachers &the Teaching Process
nodes.description: Introduce the responsibilities of the teaching profession. Students observe and learn about different models of teaching while gaining teaching experience. Students become familiar with creating classroom environments that support all students in learning. Prerequisites: EDSP 1050; EDFS 1020 or EDFS 3030; EDEC 1630, HDF 1050, or PSYS 2400. Co-requisites: EDEL 2570. Pre/Co-requisites: EDSP 1050; EDFS 1020 or EDFS 3030; EDEC 1630, HDF 1050, or PSYS 2400.
nodes.other_requirements: school sites on 10 Thursdays; 2nd or 3rd year Elementary Education (ELK6) majors only; Taken concurrently with EDEL 2570 or EDEL 3760. Lab fee: $40.00. Class meets in designated off campus elementary

nodes.title: Lab Experience in Inquiry
nodes.description: Supervised practicum in field sites. Implementation of teaching methods from Inquiry Block. Documentation of classroom work, child study, and development of portfolio. Prerequisite: Admission to Elementary Teacher Education Program; concurrent with EDEL 3570, EDEL 3580, EDEL 1590.
nodes.other_requirements: Admission to Elementary Teacher Education Program; ELK6 3rd yr, 4th yr; Admit to Teacher Education; 2.75 overall GPA requirement; Class meets in designated off campus elementary school sites and on campus; Lab fee: $40.00

nodes.title: Lab Experience in Literacy
nodes.description: Supervised practicum in a field site. Implementation of teaching methods from Literacy Block. Documentation of classroom work, child study, and development of portfolio. Prerequisite: Admission to Elementary Teacher Education Program; concurrent with EDEL 3780, EDEL 3760, EDEL 2570.
nodes.other_requirements: Admission to Elementary Teacher Education Program; ELK6 3rd yr, 4th yr; Admit to Teacher Education; 2.75 overall GPA requirement; Class meets in designated off campus elementary school sites and on campus; Lab fee: $40.00

nodes.title: Human Development
nodes.description: A comprehensive survey of life span individual and family development within social and historical context.

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission only. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Teaching Math for Meaning
nodes.description: Methods of teaching mathematics in elementary school. Research base for how children learn mathematics and how math curriculum is organized. Special focus on teaching diverse groupings of learners. Prerequisite: Admission to Elementary Teacher Education Program; concurrent with EDEL 3750, EDEL 3760, EDEL 2570.
nodes.other_requirements: Admission to Elementary Teacher Education Program; ELK6 3rd yr, 4th yr; Admit to Teacher Education; 2.75 overall GPA requirement

nodes.title: Mtg Needs of Diverse Learners
nodes.description: Designed to familiarize students with different ways that that students learn. Supports educators' responsibility to create learning environments where all students are engaged and have equitable access to learning opportunities. Prerequisites: EDSP 1050; EDFS 1020 or EDFS 3030; EDEC 1630, HDF 1050, or PSYS 2400. Co-requisites: EDEL 2550. Pre/co-requisites: EDSP 1050; EDFS 1020 or EDFS 3030; EDEC 1630, HDF 1050, or PSYS 2400.
nodes.other_requirements: 2nd or 3rd Year Elementary Education (ELK6); Taken concurrently with EDEL 2550 and EDEL 3760

nodes.title: Children's Lit & Literacy
nodes.description: Learning about the breadth of literature available for use in elementary school. Developing the ability to evaluate and use literature in reading and writing activities. Emphasis on bias-free methods. Pre/co-requisites: Admission to Elementary Teacher Education Program; concurrent with EDEL 3780, EDEL 3750 and EDEL 3760.
nodes.other_requirements: Admission to Elementary Teacher Education Program; concurrent with EDEL 3780, EDEL 3750 and EDEL 3760. ELK6 3rd yr, 4th yr; Admit to Teacher Education; 2.75 overall GPA requirement; Concurrent withEDEL 3780,EDEL 3750andEDEL 4760.

nodes.title: Internship: Student Teaching
nodes.description: Supervised student teaching internship in approved K-6 field site with an endorsed Elementary Education mentor. Semester long immersion culminating in a two week solo experience. Prerequisites: 4th yr Elementary Education (Grades K-6) major; admit to Student Teaching; EDEL 4760; overall GPA requirement and professional course GPA requirement (EDEL/EDSP/EDTE/EDML) of at least 3. Co-requisite: EDEL 4880.
nodes.other_requirements: 4th yr Elementary Education (Grades K-6) major; admit to Student Teaching; overall GPA requirement and professional course GPA requirement (EDEL/EDSP/EDTE/EDML) of at least 3. ELK6 4th yr.; Admit to Stdnt Tching; Concurrent with EDEL 4880.

nodes.title: School and Society
nodes.description: As a foundational course in the College of Education and Social Services, this course investigates the interplay between schooling and society. We examine how children and youth are situated within the context of systems, including their families, communities, and the larger society, that influence their development and learning. We will explore the sociological purposes of schools, their historical development in the US, and the resulting inequitable impact of school organization and policy.

nodes.title: Iss Aff Persons W/Disabil
nodes.description: Students study the effects of discrimination, advocacy, litigation and sociological perspectives on disabilities. History, current legislation, and family issues for children and adults are emphasized.
nodes.other_requirements: Only open to CESS first-time first-years or by instructor permission; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Developmental Psych: Childhood
nodes.description: Survey of research and theories on child development from conception to adolescence emphasizing experimental analyses of early social and cognitive development. Prerequisite: PSYS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: PSYS majors and minors only; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Student Teaching Seminar
nodes.description: Designed to enhance elementary education licensure candidates' experience as full-time interns in K-6 classrooms. Candidates will learn to create productive classroom environments that support the social, emotional, and academic growth of all students. Prerequisites: EDEL 4760; Elementary Education (Grades K-6) major; Senior standing; admit to Student Teaching. Co-requisite: EDEL 4991.
nodes.other_requirements: Elementary Education (Grades K-6) major; Senior standing; admit to Student Teaching. Concurrent with EDEL 4991
